name: Amprolium + Sulfaquinoxaline + Vitamin K (Veterinary) | Indications, Pharmacology, Dosage, Side Effects & other Generic info with Available Brand names in Bangladesh
indications: Poultry: For prevention and treatment of acute and chronic coccidiosis of poultry.Livestock (calf/goat/Sheep): For prevention and treatment of coccidiosis.
composition: Each gm powder contains- Amprolium USP 102 mg Sulfaquinoxaline Sodium 102 mg Vitamin K USP 0.1 mg
dosage_&amp;_administration: Poultry: Prevention: 1 gm/2 L drinking water for 7 days. Treatment: 1 gm/L drinking water for 5-7 days. Calf, Goat Sheep: 1.5 gm/10-15 kg body weight with water or milk for 5 days. Or as directed by veterinary physician.
interaction: With medicine: Exogenous administration of thiamine in high dosages may reverse or reduce the efficacy of amprolium. With food other: Not known.
contraindications: Contraindicated to animals with impaired liver and/or renal function hypersensitivity to amprolium and/or sulfaquinoxaline.
side_effects: Common side effects: Occasionally may cause GI or neurologic effects. Rare side effects: At high dosages in laying-hens egg-drop and in broilers growth inhibition and polyneuritis can occur.
pregnancy_&amp;_lactation: This preparation may be used in pregnant lactating animals (goat sheep) after considering the risk benefits.
precautions_&amp;_warnings: May cause thiamine deficiency if given at high rates.Withdrawal Period Poultry: Meat- 10 days, Egg- 10 days Livestock: Meat- 10 days
storage_conditions: Store in a cool and dry place, protected from light. Keep all medicines out of reach of children.
